headline: Israel Closes Airspace and Ben Gurion Airport Indefinitely
summary: Israeli Airports Authority suspended all flights and closed Israeli airspace due to ongoing Iranian ballistic missile strikes on central Israel. El Al evacuated its Boeing 787 and 737 fleet from Ben Gurion Airport, breaking Shabbat protocols. Airspace not expected to reopen until at least March 2, with controlled reopening plan allowing only one aircraft per hour initially. Over 20,000 travelers affected by cancellations across the region including UAE airports.

secondary_markets.name: El Al Israel Airlines
secondary_markets.reason: Full flight suspension through March 14+; fleet dispersed to foreign airports; bookings closed through March 21.

secondary_markets.name: Maersk
secondary_markets.reason: Halted Haifa port calls citing Israel-Iran safety risks; re-routing adds cost and transit time for Eastern Mediterranean services.

secondary_markets.name: Brent Crude Oil
secondary_markets.reason: Oil surged above $100/bbl as Strait of Hormuz near-closure removes 20M bbl/day capacity from market; Iran mining the strait compounds supply shock.

secondary_markets.name: Hapag-Lloyd
secondary_markets.reason: Suspended Strait of Hormuz operations; pending $4.2B ZIM acquisition faces wartime integration risk and Israeli port disruption.

secondary_markets.name: CMA CGM
secondary_markets.reason: Issued fresh guidance suspending Strait of Hormuz transits; Eastern Mediterranean and Gulf container services severely disrupted.

trade_position: Long war-risk insurance-exposed reinsurers (Lancashire Holdings LRE.L, Hiscox HSX.L): US/UK/Israeli-linked vessels now paying 3x normal war-risk premiums with some underwriters refusing to quote entirely — this reprices the entire Middle East marine book upward, a windfall for those still writing policies.
